title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL cursor close failure can mask permanent schema errors
summary: Oracle MySQL cursor cleanup failures could mask the original permanent write error.
observed: A schema-related write failure could be replaced by a cursor close failure, hiding the human-readable table-shape guidance.
expected: Cleanup failures must not mask original write failures.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for cursor close error masking.
impact: Operators could lose the actionable unknown-column or schema mismatch message.
delivery_semantics: The fix preserves the original failed-write classification and rollback behavior.
security: The cleanup warning remains sanitized and avoids payload or secret exposure.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL cursor close failures are logged only.
The original permanent schema error remains visible to the caller.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and Oracle MySQL sink write tests.
documentation: No separate user documentation is required beyond release-note mention of the error handling fix.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the cleanup fix is published.
